summary: Calibration ensures accurate and consistent testosterone measurements by validating instrument performance; failure to calibrate properly can lead to measurable variability in results due to incorrect analyte detection thresholds. This makes it a primary factor influencing test reliability rather than other sources like sample handling or environmental conditions.
